title: U.S. aluminum prices hit record highs amid tariffs, low supply
summary: U.S. aluminum prices have surged 40% since tariffs were raised to 50%, with the Midwest premium hitting a record high due to sharply falling inventories. Global supply shortages and higher LME prices are exacerbating the tariff impacts, threatening manufacturers and potentially fueling inflation. President Trump doubled tariffs on aluminum imports to encourage local production.

title: Aluminum Prices Soar to Four-Year High, Creating a Divide in U.S. Industry
summary: LME aluminum futures have reached four-year highs not seen since April 2022, with U.S. regional premiums exceeding $1 per pound, benefiting upstream producers like Alcoa while straining downstream manufacturers and consumers. Goldman Sachs raised its 2026 price forecast to $3,150 per ton amid tight global supply. Downstream sectors are reducing production, redesigning products, or switching to alternatives amid rising costs and job threats.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast, pushing it above $4000/t
summary: ING Group has revised its aluminum price forecast above $4,000 per tonne due to the Strait of Hormuz closure amid escalating geopolitical conflict. The disruption is impacting global supply chains for aluminum. This reflects ongoing market volatility from regional tensions.

title: The 2026 Aluminum Crisis: Hormuz Blockade Halts Gulf Swing Smelters and prices skyrocketing
summary: A Hormuz blockade has halted Gulf aluminum smelters, redrawing global production maps and causing prices to skyrocket while diverting raw materials to China at low costs. This has exposed fragile supply chains, prompting shifts to friendshoring, domestic U.S. production like a new smelter proposal, and weaponized trade policies including tariffs. Electricity costs critical for smelters are highlighted as a key profitability factor.

title: How the Iran war is squeezing metals markets and key industries
summary: The war in Iran is impacting metals markets, including aluminum, which hit a 4-year high last week due to disruptions in Middle East supplies accounting for 21% of unwrought and 13% of wrought aluminum imports. Regional producers like Aluminium Bahrain and others face challenges with alumina supply, potentially halting smelters for up to 12 months if disrupted. However, Chinese control over global aluminum prices may limit sustained highs.

title: [SMM Aluminium Analysis]Domestic Aluminum Production Rises 2.1% YoY, Liquid Aluminum Proportion to Rebound in March 2026
summary: Domestic aluminum production in China for February 2026 rose 2.1% year-over-year but fell 8.9% month-over-month over 28 days. Factors affecting production are noted, with expectations for a rebound in the liquid aluminum proportion in March 2026.

title: Aluminium prices surge, supplies to America and Europe threatened
summary: Aluminum prices are surging due to the closure of the Qatalum smelter in Qatar caused by gas disruptions. This raises concerns over global aluminum supply chains, particularly affecting deliveries to America and Europe.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast, pushing it above $4000/t
summary: ING Group has revised its aluminum price forecast upward to above USD 4000 per tonne due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating geopolitical conflict in the Middle East. This disruption threatens key supply routes for aluminum production.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: The IBAAS 2026 conference in India will unite global aluminium experts to focus on bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, AI, and sustainable practices. Key themes include bauxite beneficiation, red mud management, advanced alloys, and digitalization for Industry 4.0. It addresses decarbonization strategies and circular economy initiatives across the aluminium value chain.

title: Century Aluminum Backs New U.S. Smelter Built Around Next-Generation Smelting Technology
summary: Century Aluminum is partnering with Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) to build a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma, using EGA's next-generation EX smelting technology. This facility aims to produce 750,000 metric tons annually, reducing energy use and emissions while doubling U.S. smelting capacity. Construction starts in 2026, supporting jobs and domestic supply for key industries.

title: Aluminium Giants Hit Major Milestone with Low-Carbon Production
summary: Elysis, a joint venture of Alcoa and Rio Tinto, has installed its carbon-free inert anode technology in a full-scale 450-kA cell, a worldwide first for the aluminium industry. This advances low-carbon aluminium production, building on pilots used in Apple products and Audi wheels. The technology can retrofit existing smelters, aiding decarbonization efforts.

title: Aluminum at the Centre of Global Industrial Transformation AICE 2026
summary: AICE 2026 highlights aluminium's role amid regulatory pressures, focusing on recycling, circular economy, and sustainable production technologies. It addresses energy-intensive primary production challenges through scrap collection, sorting, and remelting innovations. The event fosters dialogue on emissions reduction, transparency, and closed-loop systems for construction and automotive sectors.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The aluminum scrap market is booming due to policy shifts, rising prices, and demand for sustainability, with recycled aluminum using 95% less energy than primary production. Innovations in closed-loop recycling, green smelting, and sorting support circular economy goals in construction, automotive, and electronics. Asia-Pacific leads demand, aiding global climate targets.

title: Aluminium-Free High-Barrier Films Market to Reach USD 3.82 Billion by 2035 as Sustainable Packaging Replaces Foil-Based Structures
summary: The market for aluminum-free high-barrier films is growing rapidly, driven by sustainable packaging innovations like recyclable mono-materials and advanced coatings. Companies such as Constantia Flexibles and Jindal Poly Films are investing in technologies that replace aluminum foil with eco-friendly alternatives. These developments reduce environmental impact while maintaining barrier performance for food packaging.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,148 CNY/T on March 20, 2026, up 0.48% from the previous day, with a 3.79% increase over the past month despite being 0.25% lower year-over-year. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,140 per ton to six-week highs following China's pledge to curb excess capacity. Futures held steady above CNY 3,070 per ton near one-month highs amid commitments to reduce overcapacity, though mills face oversupply and export constraints.

title: US flat steel pricing continues climb, lower March scrap could be balanced by low steel imports
summary: Weekly US flat steel prices continued ascending, with hot-rolled coils up another $10/nt amid solid demand. Shredded scrap rose $30/gt, potentially finishing near $395-405/gt, while lower March scrap is offset by reduced steel imports. Recent updates note US flat steel prices mostly higher on March 20 amid demand and geopolitical risks.

title: CRU: Fewer imports tighten US steel supply, supporting domestic prices
summary: Lower finished steel imports supported US domestic prices, with HR coil prices up over $40/mt month-on-month due to seasonal demand and tighter supply from S232 tariffs and AD/CVD duties. Rebar market saw gains from fully booked producers and rising scrap prices, though higher US prices may attract more imports. Long products prices rose amid tight supply, data centers, infrastructure demand, and limited imports.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: In early March 2026, 3/4-inch steel pipe prices increased 7% to 10% nationally due to rising demand from data centers and spring construction season ramp-up. Domestic raw steel production rose 6.2% year-over-year in the week ending February 28, with year-to-date up 5% and higher capability utilization.

title: Q1 2026 Metal Pricing Trends: What Contractors Must Know
summary: March 2026 metal market update highlights rising steel mill output and strong demand as construction season approaches. Discussion covers steel alongside aluminum trends, tariffs, global events impacting supply, and advice for contractors on escalation clauses and lead times amid capacity constraints.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues climbing above $1,000, driven by trade restrictions limiting North American supply and ongoing market pressures.

title: AISI: Raw steel production climbs to four-year high
summary: US mills produced 1,817,000 short tons of raw steel in the week ending February 21, reaching the highest level in over four years. Mill capability utilization increased to 78.5%, and year-to-date production rose 4.3% compared to the same period last year.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: Year-to-date steel production through February 28 rose 5% from the previous year with increased capability utilization. Steel pipe prices increased 7-10% nationally in early March due to higher demand from data centers and spring construction season activity.

title: Steel Imports Up 4.6% In January 2026 vs. December 2025
summary: The US imported 1,650,000 net tons of steel in January 2026, with finished steel imports up 7.7% versus December 2025. However, total and finished steel imports remain down significantly compared to January 2025, declining 46.3% and 45.9% respectively.

title: Steel Consuming Market Outlook March 2026
summary: Construction planning is cooling in early 2026 but remains at historically high levels. Residential housing construction is rebounding with January 2026 housing starts up 9.5% year-over-year, driven by steel-intensive multi-family projects.

title: Most of copper's rally has happened, Goldman Sachs says. They fear a correction is coming
summary: Goldman Sachs predicts an 18% drop in copper prices to $11,000 per ton by December 2026 due to a growing surplus and easing fundamentals, despite raising price estimates by 22%. The recent 23% rally since November was largely driven by speculative inflows, with manufacturers switching to alternatives like aluminum. Global inventories are building ahead of Lunar New Year restocking.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed — March 19, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices fell up to $0.19 per pound across major grades, with Comex copper settling at $5.48 per pound, down 4.9%, and LME 3-month copper at $12,395.50 per ton, down 2.97%. The declines align with macro pressures from a stronger US dollar, rising energy costs, and geopolitical disruptions impacting demand. Other metals like brass saw smaller drops, while aluminum and steel held steady.

title: Copper Price Today: Metal Retracts as Geopolitical Tensions Ease and Inventories Build
summary: Copper is trading at $5.95 per pound, down 0.71% over the last seven days, entering consolidation after hitting all-time highs above $6.11 earlier in 2026. Prices eased due to fading geopolitical risk premiums from de-escalated tensions over Greenland and rising LME inventories to highest since May 2025. Technically, support is at $5.80-$5.90, with long-term bullish trends from AI and green energy demand.

title: Copper Futures Decline Across Contracts in March 2026 Session
summary: Copper futures traded lower, with the most active May 2026 contract settling at $5.3845 per pound, down $0.2095, amid lower trading volume and open interest. Prices for contracts into 2027 and 2028 also declined. The session reflects ongoing market pressures in March 2026.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices averaging $5.17/lb in 2026, up from $4.82/lb in 2025, amid a narrowing surplus; other analysts like Citigroup predict above $5.90/lb by Q2 2026. China copper prices fell in March due to rising SHFE and LME inventories up 56% month-on-month and a firmer US dollar. The 2025 market saw a 94,000t surplus in the first three quarters.

title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
summary: Global copper supply faces a structural deficit, with prices soaring to $12,000-$14,500 per metric ton on the LME due to mine disruptions like the shuttered Cobre Panama mine. China's top smelters agreed to cut 2026 production by over 10% amid collapsing treatment charges from ore shortages. This crunch impacts renewable energy sectors, squeezing margins for wind turbine makers like Vestas.

title: Copper Drops to 3-Month Low
summary: Copper futures fell below $5.4 per pound, the lowest in three months, amid a strong dollar and concerns over high energy prices denting manufacturing demand. The decline tracks broader industrial metals weakness following Persian Gulf conflict disruptions. Copper is headed for a third weekly drop despite some rebound, with surging inventories signaling softer demand.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: The Copper for Tomorrow bid seeks to address challenges in the copper value chain to boost sustainable production amid rising demand for clean energy transition. It focuses on processing lower-grade ores with less energy, water, and waste, while electrifying mining operations. The initiative unites industry and researchers to meet ESG goals and double global production sustainably over the next 30 years.

title: 'An immediate transition to copper is technically and economically feasible'
summary: Rising silver prices are prompting large solar manufacturers to switch to copper for cell metallization, with experts predicting industry-wide adoption. Performance of copper-metallized modules matches silver when properly implemented, though silver will remain for specific uses. Researchers emphasize silver thrifting and alternative pastes to cut costs without reliability risks.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: Innovations in copper mining include reprocessing waste stockpiles, with Freeport-McMoRan targeting 360,000 tpa from chalcopyrite waste using advanced leaching. Amerigo generates revenue from El Teniente mine waste, equivalent to a 30,000 tpa mine. New flowsheets could cut water use by 78%, emphasizing water's value in sustainable operations.

title: Gunnison Copper COO on 2026 Copper Production Goals
summary: Gunnison Copper aims for two million pounds of monthly production using oxide and Tom leach systems at its Arizona projects. Partnerships with Rio Tinto's Newton and Amazon Web Services integrate its copper into U.S. supply chains and data centers. Recent debt reduction and an upcoming RPA update position the company for strong 2026 growth.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Futures Price Falls to US$17,165/Ton (Wednesday, March 18, 2026)
summary: The 3-month nickel commodity futures price fell to US$17,165 per ton on March 18, 2026, down from US$17,420 the previous day. Over the last week, prices declined by 1.77% with an average daily trading price of US$17,440 per ton, though prices remain up 1.48% compared to the start of 2026.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Global nickel prices are currently flat at $16,853.62 per ton amid a tug-of-war between macroeconomic headwinds and supply constraints. Indonesia's 34% cut to annual nickel ore production quotas and resilient downstream demand from Chinese stainless steel mills and EV battery manufacturers are providing market support, though analyst forecasts cluster between $15,000 and $16,000 per tonne.

title: SMM Nickel Midday Review - March 23, 2026
summary: On March 23, nickel prices rose slightly as geopolitical tensions influenced market sentiment, with Trump demanding that Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours. This news item indicates ongoing price volatility driven by both supply-side factors and macroeconomic developments.

title: TECH Project high-grade nickel is primed to fuel the global green transition
summary: Queensland Pacific Metals’ TECH Project aims to become the world’s premier source of high-grade, ethically derived nickel for EV batteries and green energy. The project features the innovative DNi Process, which recycles over 98% of nitric acid, requires no tailings dams, and produces minimal waste, making it carbon-negative and sustainable. General Motors has secured an option to purchase all uncommitted nickel and cobalt production for the first 15 years.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries Limited reported zero lost-time injuries and 17.7 million safe man-hours in its quarterly update, emphasizing workforce safety as key to sustainable operations. The company advanced sustainability through a strategic investment in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt and received awards for ESG leadership. It also secured an offtake agreement highlighting product quality for global supply chains.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices face volatility in 2026 due to global oversupply and rising EV demand, with battery-grade nickel expected to triple by 2030. Alaska Energy Metals' Nikolai Project in Alaska advances as a key US domestic sulphide nickel resource, supported by FAST-41 permitting for infrastructure upgrades. This aligns with efforts to reshore critical minerals for national security and clean energy.

title: Inside a $1.1B deal to reshore critical minerals refining
summary: Nth Cycle's electrochemical refining technology for nickel, cobalt, and other minerals from battery scrap enables a $1.1B deal with Trafigura to expand US capacity fourfold. The compact system reduces capital costs and waste compared to traditional refineries, addressing reliance on Indonesia and China. It processes black mass from recycled batteries, keeping valuable resources domestic.

title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
summary: Tenaris is showcasing its portfolio of hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy (March 24-27). The company highlights its integrated European supply chain, with the Piombino facility playing a central role in providing galvanized, epoxy-coated, and polyethylene-coated pipes with recent Environmental Product Declaration certifications.
